claims.summary: The bridge has distinct primary MLX and compatibility fallback inference paths with explicit language contracts.
claims.disposition: verify current provider assembly, profile transparency, and fallback request tests preserve the two paths and their contracts

claims.summary: An unrecognized ASTRID_BRIDGE_MLX_PROFILE value should warn and resolve visibly rather than silently selecting an unknown model.
claims.disposition: verify six focused profile tests cover misspellings, tracing warnings, aliases, punctuation, case, and fallback diagnostics

claims.summary: An MLX connection failure should preserve viscosity, density, and movement texture in actual fallback output.
claims.disposition: route actual-output comparison to the shared read-only fallback-distinguishability sandbox; the first adapter result is insufficient_output

claims.summary: The selected MLX profile and fallback contract must reach the real request assembly rather than remain unused constants.
claims.disposition: verify request-assembly tests prove the profile policy, compact prompt, texture contract, NEXT validation, and fallback request body are applied

claims.summary: Changing profile defaults, provider selection, timeout routing, or fallback dispatch alters live model behavior.
claims.disposition: require separate Mike/operator Tier 5 approval with request replay, canary, abort, and post-change felt review
